fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: Minnesota Occupational Therapist License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a occupational therapist? Here's whether your license transfers to Minnesota - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: occupational therapist license reciprocity minnesota, how to transfer occupational therapist license to minnesota, occupational therapist compact minnesota, minnesota occupational therapist endorsement application

board.name: Minnesota Board of Medical Practice — OT

quick_facts.exam_name: National Board for Certification in Occupational Therapy (NBCOT) exam

license_types.title: Occupational Therapist Registered (OTR)
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice occupational therapy independently in Minnesota.

license_types.title: Certified Occupational Therapy Assistant (COTA)
license_types.description: Requires an associate degree and practicing under the supervision of a licensed OTR.

steps.title: Graduate from an Accredited Program
steps.description: Complete an occupational therapy educational program accredited by the Accreditation Council for Occupational Therapy Education (ACOTE).

steps.title: Pass the NBCOT Exam
steps.description: Successfully pass the National Board for Certification in Occupational Therapy (NBCOT) exam.

steps.title: Apply for a License
steps.description: Submit an application to the Minnesota Board of Occupational Therapy, including required documentation and fees.

documents.detail: Sent directly from your educational institution.
Official verification of passing the NBCOT exam.

provisional_text: Many states offer a limited permit or provisional license for new graduates waiting to take the NBCOT exam. Check specific board rules for supervisory requirements.
reciprocity_text: Occupational therapists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current NBCOT certification.

faqs.question: How much does a Minnesota OT license cost?
faqs.answer: The initial application and licensing fee varies, often around  to . Check the specific fee breakdown.

faqs.question: Is NBCOT certification required?
faqs.answer: Yes, passing the NBCOT exam is a standard requirement for initial licensure across all states.

faqs.question: Does Minnesota have reciprocity for occupational therapists?
faqs.answer: Minnesota has enacted the OT Compact, but compact privileges are not yet issuing. For now, occupational therapists still apply by endorsement through the state board.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my occupational therapist license to Minnesota?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 Weeks. If you qualify for an active compact privilege, there may be no separate wait to start practicing.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Minnesota while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Occupational therapists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current NBCOT certification.

hero.title: Become a Occupational Therapist in Minnesota
hero.dek: Minnesota Occupational Therapist license requires graduation from an ACOTE-accredited program, passing the NBCOT exam, and a background check.
